title: rubus argenteus subsp. castranus
intro: Rubus argenteus subsp. castranus, also known as the silver bramble, is a subspecies of the raspberry plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of flavonoids and ellagic acid, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Rubus argenteus subsp. castranus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of menopausal disorders.
